description: Validation matrix for canonical n/N progress semantics in A2A Terminal Brief aggregation. Ensures completed/total counts reflect canonical task outcomes, not lane order, event sequence, origin-local projection count, retries, duplicates, out-of-order delivery, or transient state.

scenarios.name: canonical-task-completed-count
scenarios.description: n/N uses completed canonical child tasks as numerator, total canonical child tasks as denominator. Not lane order, event sequence, or origin-local projection count.

then.reason: Only tasks with succeeded terminal status count toward completed. Lane order (task-1 first, task-3 third, task-5 fifth) is irrelevant; event sequence (which task reported terminal first) is irrelevant; origin-projection-count (which broker projected) is irrelevant.

scenarios.name: retry-superseded-original-does-not-inflate-completed
scenarios.description: When a canonical task is retried (original fails, retry succeeds), completed counts only the terminal success once. The original failed attempt is superseded and must not inflate completed.

then.detail: task-a succeeded after retry. The failed attempt (attempt 1) is superseded. Only one canonical task completion is counted for task-a. task-b succeeded on first attempt. Both count as 1 each.

scenarios.failsIf: completed would be 1 (if original retry edge-case conflated) or 3 (if both succeeded and superseded failed were counted separately)

scenarios.name: duplicate-replay-does-not-inflate-completed
scenarios.description: Replaying the same terminal event (duplicate delivery, broker restart replay, outbox reprocess) must not increment the completed count.

then.detail: task-x and task-y each succeeded once. The replay events with the same deliveryId are idempotently suppressed and must not increment completed.

scenarios.name: failed-cancelled-do-not-count-as-completed
scenarios.description: Canonical tasks that end in failed, cancelled, or blocked state are NOT counted as completed. They are still part of total.

then.detail: Only task-p and task-s succeeded (completed = 2). task-q failed (not completed), task-r canceled (not completed). All 4 are part of total.

scenarios.failsIf: completed = 3 or 4 (failed/cancelled leaked into completed) or completed = 1 (succeeded count wrong)

scenarios.name: out-of-order-completion-count-is-order-independent
scenarios.description: Completed count is independent of the order in which tasks reach terminal state. Tasks completing late arrive at the same count as tasks completing early.

then.detail: Whether tasks complete in order A (o, m, n), order B (n, o, m), or any other order, the completed count is always 3/3.

scenarios.failsIf: completed differs between order A and order B, or completed reflects sequence position instead of canonical task count

scenarios.name: single-task-1-1-fallback
scenarios.description: A parent round with exactly one canonical child task renders 1/1 when that task completes.

then.detail: 1/1 is valid and expected for a single-task round. The denominator reflects that there is one canonical task.

scenarios.failsIf: completed = 0 (task succeeded but not counted), total = 0 (total missing), or n/N omitted because N=1

scenarios.name: cross-broker-aggregation-counts-canonical-tasks-not-projections
scenarios.description: In cross-broker parent-round aggregation, the completed/total counts reflect canonical child tasks under the child broker of record, not the number of projection entries in the parent broker's ledger.

then.detail: There are 3 canonical child tasks (2 under broker A, 1 under broker B), each succeeded. Completed = 3 across brokers. Parent projection count is also 3 in this case but the projection count could differ from completed (e.g., if a task has multiple projections for different terminal kinds).

scenarios.failsIf: completed reflects projection count instead of canonical child task count, or brokA-projection-count is used instead of cross-broker aggregate

scenarios.name: sessionKey-redacted-from-progress-evidence
scenarios.description: sessionKey values must be redacted from n/N progress evidence. The completed/total counts do not include session identifiers; sessionKey is not used in n/N computation.

then.detail: completed = 2 regardless of sessionKey values. sessionKey must not appear in title, body summary, or evidence URLs of Terminal Brief notifications.

scenarios.failsIf: sessionKey appears in title, n/N text, or evidence summaries of the Terminal Brief notification

scenarios.name: ack-read-visibility-boundary
scenarios.description: n/N progress is a terminal evidence projection, not a terminal ACK. It does not prove provider delivery, operator read, human visibility, or terminal-outbox ACK.

then.detail: n/N = 2/2 is an evidence projection of completed canonical child tasks. It must not be treated as ACK/read/visibility/approval. Provider accepted-send evidence is send-acceptance only.

scenarios.failsIf: n/N notification is treated as terminal ACK, read receipt, or operator approval for deploy/restart/send/DB-mutation actions

scenarios.name: go-gate-safety-boundary
scenarios.description: Source-only GO for this validation matrix does not authorize production deploy, Gateway restart, live provider send, terminal-outbox ACK, database mutation, secret rotation, or repository visibility change.

then.detail: This validation matrix is a source-only reading. No step creates, deploys, restarts, sends, or mutates anything. Provider accepted-send evidence is send-acceptance only.

scenarios.failsIf: any test in this matrix triggers a production deploy, restart, live send, DB mutation, ACK, or secret operation
